changedFiles.reason: Declared installer integrity, release documentation, and critical coverage scope.
Recorded verification and patch-release residual risk.
Added pre-write managed conflicts and post-install content/import/Make validation for every install.
Covered multi-conflict failure, no writes, import failure, Make failure, and rollback behavior.
Corrected public adoption capability and documented critical per-file coverage floors.
Corrected the stale release claim and Japanese spacing while documenting coverage floors.
Corrected the stale release claim and documented critical per-file coverage floors.
Corrected Japanese product and PR-level spacing.
Enforced synchronized multilingual release capability markers and Japanese spacing.
Covered Checkpoint main flow and Finish evidence/failure helpers directly.
Generated coverage JSON and enforced critical per-file regression floors.
Added per-file coverage floors for nine lifecycle-critical modules.
Covered accepted, missing, and regressed per-file coverage reports.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Never report installation success when managed runtime files are incomplete or inconsistent.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: All installs validate managed bytes, runtime imports, and Make parsing before reporting completion.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Detect conflicts before the first write and list actionable repository-relative paths.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Fresh-install conflicts are enumerated from the same copy pairs and fail before transaction setup.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Preserve explicitly project-owned files and idempotent identical content.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Glossary and existing-install stack policy remain unchanged; byte-identical managed files are allowed.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Published capability statements must derive from release metadata and remain synchronized across languages.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Three READMEs carry the same machine-checked adoption/SHA256 capability marker.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Do not hand-edit generated cockpit status.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Status was generated by lifecycle commands.

risk.detail: Installer ownership and validation changes affect clean installs, reinstalls, force installs, and upgrades.

observedIssues: Existing conflicting managed scripts previously produced a partial successful installation.
All three READMEs retained a pre-v0.5.0 adoption capability statement after release.
Overall coverage alone did not prevent lifecycle-critical module regressions.

residualRisks.detail: The immutable v0.5.0 tag predates these fixes; publish a new patch tag rather than moving v0.5.0.
Checkpoint reached 87% and Finish 39%; Scope, Status Generation, Summary, Archive, Review Policy, and Status Consistency remain below 70% but now have regression floors.
Minimal/core/full installation modes and a one-page onboarding path remain unimplemented.
Nine Preset-only stacks still lack real-project CI evidence.

reviewReadiness.reason: Both P1 defects are fixed with regression tests; critical coverage has direct tests and per-file floors, but publication requires a new patch release.

userCorrectionsCaptured: Address the v0.5.0 engineering review without overstating production readiness.
userCorrectionSolidification: Installer completion now requires verified runtime integrity; release capability markers are machine checked.

checkpointEvidence.detail: Partial install reproduction, file ownership, rollback, stale release claims, and coverage baseline reviewed.
113 tests, 65.68 percent total coverage, nine critical floors, installer conflict/import/Make tests, docs, and v0.5.0 remote contract reviewed.

knownGaps: A new patch tag is required for public delivery.
Several lifecycle-critical modules remain below the long-term 70 percent target.
Preset-only real-project CI and reduced installation modes remain future work.
overclaimPrevention: Do not describe v0.5.0 as containing these post-tag fixes or per-file floors as meeting the long-term 70 percent target.
